mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates rapidly, triggering wage growth expectations and consumer spending acceleration that lifts equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized private-sector demand expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95.0 indicates capacity utilization approaching full employment, which historically precedes margin compression and equity drawdowns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of observations where INDPRO sustains levels above 95.0, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2015 data.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI exceeds 2.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, forcing capital into equities and generating short-term positive returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative in more than 50% of instances meeting the dual threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates consistently above 2.0 percent reflect underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenue forecasts, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, or the signal generates losses in more than 55% of positive GDP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in real consumer credit (RSAFS) indicate deteriorating household balance sheets, which suppress aggregate demand and drive negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the joint signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance level (50%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing 96.0 while housing starts fall below 500 signals supply-demand imbalance favoring producers, which boosts industrial and financial sector earnings and lifts the S&P 500 within one month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered windows, or the cross-sectional rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year period.
